title: Memory Capacity Production Slowly Expanding To Meet AI Industry Demand
gist: Samsung and SK Hynix are jointly investing 800 trillion Won ($518B) to build four new memory fabs plus an HBM packaging hub in Korea's Southwest Region, while SK Hynix separately builds a $4B HBM facility in Indiana and Micron invests $3B in the US supply chain; DRAM and NAND makers are reportedly sold out into 2027, but significant new capacity isn't expected until late 2027 or 2028.

insights.claim: Samsung and SK Hynix are jointly investing a combined 800 trillion Won ($518 billion) to build four new memory manufacturing sites plus a high-bandwidth memory (HBM) packaging hub in Korea's Southwest Region, announced alongside Korea's President Lee Jae Myung; together the two companies produce about two-thirds of the world's DRAM.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A $518B joint Korean memory investment (unprecedented in scale) is the single largest capacity-expansion data point for the DRAM/HBM supply chain currently constraining AI accelerator production — critical for modeling when memory bottlenecks to AI datacenter buildout ease.
insights.quote: Samsung and SK hynix are investing a combined 800 trillion Won ($518B) to build four new memory manufacturing sites and a high-bandwidth memory, HBM, packing hub in Korea's Southwest Region to meet AI driven demand.

insights.claim: DRAM and NAND manufacturers have been reporting they are sold out at least into 2027, causing prices to skyrocket and creating a serious constraint on the rollout of new AI data centers and consumer applications.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Memory manufacturers being sold out through 2027 is a hard supply constraint directly gating AI datacenter buildout pace, reinforcing memory (not just GPU/logic capacity) as a binding constraint on AI infrastructure scaling — a key variable for AI capex timing models.
insights.quote: The DRAM and NAND manufacturers have been reporting that they are sold out at least into 2027. As a result, prices have skyrocketed and these products are a serious constraint on the role out of new AI data centers and consumer applications.

themes.theme: US memory capacity additions and timeline

insights.claim: SK Hynix is building a $4 billion advanced packaging and R&D facility in West Lafayette, Indiana focused on high bandwidth memory (HBM), with mass production scheduled for late 2028.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A concrete late-2028 mass-production date for SK Hynix's US HBM packaging facility is a specific milestone for when domestic US HBM packaging capacity comes online, relevant to onshoring timelines for AI accelerator supply chains.
insights.quote: This will be a $4B advanced packaging and R&D facility focusing on high bandwidth memory, HBM... Mass production is scheduled for late 2028.

insights.claim: Despite massive announced investments from Samsung, SK Hynix, Sandisk, Kioxia, and Micron (including Micron's $3 billion US supply-chain investment and a new wafer supply deal, and Sandisk/Kioxia's 10th-generation 3D NAND production launch in Japan), significant new memory manufacturing capacity is not expected until late 2027 or 2028, as current demand far outstrips supply.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Explicitly frames late-2027/2028 as the earliest realistic relief point for the current memory supply crunch, a key timing anchor for investors modeling when DRAM/NAND pricing pressure on AI hardware costs might ease.
insights.quote: However, despite these massive investments, significant new manufacturing capacity isn't expected until late 2027 or 2028, as current demand far outstrips supply.
